title: MetaTrader 5 Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)

meta_description: MetaTrader 5 Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026). MetaTrader 5 (4.5★, Free). All plans compared with hidden fees revealed. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete breakdown of MetaTrader 5 pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
MetaTrader 5 Pricing Overview

MetaTrader 5 is completely free. There is no subscription fee, no monthly charge, no premium tier, and no paywall. The platform costs $0/month to download, install, and use—whether you're a retail trader, an algorithmic trading developer, or an automated trading enthusiast.

This free model is possible because MetaQuotes Software Corp., the company behind MetaTrader 5 (founded in 2010 and headquartered in Limassol, Cyprus), generates revenue from brokers—not traders. Brokers pay MetaQuotes to license and distribute the platform to their clients. You, as a trader, get the full platform at no cost, which is why MT5 has become the world's most widely used multi-asset trading platform, trusted by over 1,000 brokers globally.

However, "free" doesn't mean there are no costs associated with trading on MetaTrader 5. Understanding the actual cost structure requires looking beyond the platform itself to the fees imposed by your broker, data providers, and any third-party tools you choose to use.

All MetaTrader 5 Plans Compared

MetaTrader 5 offers a single plan with no tiering:

Plan Price What You Get
MetaTrader 5 Platform Free ($0/month)
  • Full desktop application (Windows, Mac via Wine, Linux via Wine)
  • Mobile apps (iOS and Android)
  • Live and demo trading accounts
  • 1,000+ broker support
  • Advanced backtesting with real tick data
  • Multi-asset support: forex, stocks, futures, options, crypto
  • Expert Advisors (EAs) and MQL5 programming
  • Strategy Tester with optimization
  • Custom indicators and scripts
  • Paper trading (demo accounts)
  • Built-in alerts, news feed, social trading features
  • Performance analytics and risk management tools
  • Access to MQL5 Marketplace for community EAs and indicators

There are no upgrades, no feature tiers, and no hidden platform fees. Every trader gets access to the same professional-grade tools—from day traders to institutional algo developers.

Free Plan / Free Trial

MetaTrader 5 is entirely free—there is no trial period because there is no paid version to trial into. You download the platform directly from MetaQuotes' website or through your broker and start trading immediately on a demo (paper trading) account with virtual money. When you're ready, you can link a live trading account with your broker of choice.

Demo accounts are unlimited and unrestricted. You can use them to:

  • Learn the platform interface without risking money
  • Backtest trading strategies using real historical data
  • Develop and test Expert Advisors (automated trading bots) before deploying them live
  • Practice order entry, position management, and risk controls
  • Test different brokers' platforms before committing real capital

The demo experience is functionally identical to live trading—the only difference is the account uses virtual funds. This makes MetaTrader 5 genuinely cost-free to evaluate and use at any experience level.

Hidden Costs and Fees

While the MetaTrader 5 platform itself is free, the actual cost of trading on MT5 depends on your broker and trading style. Here are the real costs to consider:

Broker Commissions and Spreads

Every trade you execute incurs a cost determined by your broker, not MetaQuotes. This typically comes in two forms:

  • Spreads: The difference between the bid and ask price. On popular pairs like EUR/USD, spreads may be 0.1–1 pip or more depending on your broker's tier and account type. Forex scalpers and day traders feel this cost most acutely.
  • Commissions: Some brokers charge a per-trade fee ($1–$20+ per round trip) in addition to or instead of wider spreads. ECN and STP brokers typically use commission-based pricing.

MT5 supports both spread-based and commission-based brokers, so your cost structure depends entirely on your broker selection—not the platform.

Market Data Feeds

MetaTrader 5 displays real-time market data from your broker. If your broker provides delayed or limited data, your backtesting and live trading may suffer. Premium data feeds (Level 2 depth-of-market, tick-by-tick historical data, alternative assets) may carry additional costs from your broker.

Third-Party Indicators and Expert Advisors

The MQL5 Marketplace offers thousands of pre-built indicators and Expert Advisors, many of which carry one-time or subscription costs (typically $10–$500 depending on complexity and track record). While many quality tools are free, the most sophisticated automated trading algorithms often command premium prices.

VPS (Virtual Private Server) for Always-On Trading

If you run automated strategies or EAs, you may want to host MetaTrader 5 on a Virtual Private Server so your algorithms run 24/7 without relying on your personal computer. VPS costs typically range from $10–$30 per month (approximate) depending on the provider and specifications. Some brokers offer free or discounted VPS for account holders who maintain minimum deposits.

Education and Training Tools

MetaTrader 5 includes built-in educational resources, but premium courses on MQL5 programming, strategy development, or backtesting optimization may cost extra. The platform's documentation is comprehensive and free, but third-party training typically isn't.

MetaTrader 5 Pricing vs Competitors

MetaTrader 5 dominates on price because it's free, but it faces competition from other platforms with different pricing models:

Platform Base Cost Best For Key Tradeoff vs MT5
MetaTrader 5 Free Algo traders, EA developers, multi-asset traders Dated UI; MQL5 learning curve; Mac/Linux via Wine
TradingView (approx.) Free–$665/mo Charting, technical analysis, community Superior UI and charting; limited backtesting vs MT5; no native EA support (uses Pine Script)
cTrader Free Forex traders, modern interface lovers Modern UI; fewer brokers than MT5; no crypto or futures
NinjaTrader (approx.) Free–$100+/mo Futures and options traders Strong for futures; data/brokerage fees add up; primarily US-focused
Interactive Brokers Platform Free (with brokerage account) Professional traders, API access Built-in; no native backtesting like MT5; steep commissions and minimums

The verdict: MetaTrader 5 is unmatched on price. No competitor charges less ($0 is unbeatable). However, TradingView and cTrader offer more modern interfaces, while NinjaTrader dominates futures trading. The choice depends on your asset class, automation needs, and interface preferences—not just cost.

Is MetaTrader 5 Worth the Price?

For most traders, MetaTrader 5 is unquestionably worth the price because it costs nothing. The real question is: is it the right platform for your trading style and skill level?

Best For:

  • Forex traders: MT5's native support from 1,000+ brokers makes it the default choice. Spreads are tight, and the platform is optimized for FX.
  • Algorithmic traders and EA developers: MQL5 is a full object-oriented programming language. If you want to build sophisticated automated strategies, MT5 is superior to most competitors.
  • Multi-asset traders: MT5 natively supports forex, stocks, futures, options, and crypto across a single interface—few competitors offer this range.
  • Backtesting enthusiasts: The Strategy Tester with real tick data is industry-leading. You can validate strategies on years of historical data before risking capital.
  • Budget-conscious traders: If cost is a primary concern, MetaTrader 5 is free to download and use. Your only costs are broker spreads/commissions.

Consider Alternatives If:

  • You prioritize interface design and user experience. TradingView and cTrader feel more modern.
  • You trade primarily futures and options. NinjaTrader has deeper features for these assets.
  • You're on macOS or Linux and want native performance. MetaTrader 5's Wine-based support is functional but occasionally glitchy.
  • You want to avoid the MQL5 learning curve. TradingView's Pine Script is simpler for beginners.

How to Save on MetaTrader 5

MetaTrader 5 itself is free, so there are no discount codes, annual billing options, or premium tiers to upgrade. However, you can reduce your total trading costs:

  • Choose a low-spread broker: Your broker choice has the largest impact on cost. Compare spreads and commissions across multiple MetaTrader 5 brokers before opening an account.
  • Use STP/ECN brokers for lower spreads: While commission-based, these often result in lower total costs for frequent traders than market-maker brokers.
  • Seek brokers with free VPS: Many offer free Virtual Private Server access if you maintain a minimum account balance—this eliminates the $10–$30/month VPS cost.
  • Use free MQL5 resources: The community provides thousands of free indicators and EAs. You don't need to buy premium ones to start automated trading.
  • Leverage the demo account: Spend time backtesting and practicing on a demo before trading live. This zero-risk approach can save you from costly mistakes during the learning phase.
  • Automate cautiously: EAs that promise unrealistic returns often deliver losses. The MQL5 Marketplace is full of overpromising products—vet strategies carefully or code your own.

MetaTrader 5 remains the gold standard for cost-effective, professional-grade trading. At $0/month, it's a platform that scales from casual traders to institutional algo developers without any software licensing barrier.
